title: Comedy Bang! Bang! — Ground Beefing Tour 2026 (Scott Aukerman)
location: Buckhead Theatre, 3110 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Scott Aukerman brings the Comedy Bang! Bang! podcast to life with surprise guests and absurd characters. Built-in improv energy and laughing together — perfect low-effort, high-fun weeknight date.

title: Rhys Darby at Helium Comedy Club
location: Helium Comedy Club, 3070 Windward Plaza, Alpharetta, GA
description: The Flight of the Conchords / Our Flag Means Death star drops into Alpharetta for four nights. Quirky, character-driven stand-up — great if your date likes smart, weird comedy.

title: Sabor Fino Latin Dance Social at Politan Row
location: Politan Row at Colony Square, 1197 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Late-night salsa/bachata social with food-hall cocktails right there. Date-friendly format: grab dinner, then stay for the dancing. Beginner-welcome vibe, no partner required.

title: Salsa at Politan Row (Sundays)
location: Politan Row at Colony Square, 1197 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Sunday afternoon salsa & bachata in the Colony Square food hall. Casual, sunlit, and a great first-time 'dance date' — DJs play, food trucks are open, and there's room to be a beginner together.

title: Reggae on the Rooftop Festival — Juneteenth Weekend
location: Air Rooftop (formerly Suite Lounge), Atlanta, GA
description: Six hours of reggae on a rooftop for Juneteenth. Open-air, vibey, dance-floor energy — easy date flow: cocktails, golden-hour views, and a soundtrack that keeps you close.

title: Hip Hop, Afrobeat & Reggae Tunes — 80s/90s/2000s Vibez
location: King of Diamonds Studios, Forest Park, GA
description: Friday-night throwback party fusing hip-hop, Afrobeats, and reggae from three decades. High-energy crowd, all-night format — best for couples who want to dance until sunrise together.

title: Pride Night at Zoo Atlanta
location: Zoo Atlanta, 800 Cherokee Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: An after-hours, adults-friendly Pride evening at the zoo. Walks through illuminated habitats, themed entertainment — a uniquely Atlanta 'talk about it for years' date idea.

title: Jazz Brunch at C. Ellet's
location: C. Ellet's at The Battery Atlanta, 2625 Cir 75 Pkwy SE, Atlanta, GA
description: Saturday jazz brunch on The Battery patio. Live trio, cocktails, and people-watching — a low-key, romantic mid-day date before the evening events ramp up.

title: R&B Soul Brunch at City Winery Atlanta
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Vivid Events brings a soul-R&B brunch show to City Winery. Wine, brunch plates, and live vocals — an easy, all-in-one Saturday date with zero planning required.

title: Soul, Jazz + Funk Pre-Father's Day Concert
location: Mable House Barnes Amphitheatre, 5239 Floyd Rd, Mableton, GA
description: Free-spirited outdoor soul-jazz-funk show in Mableton. Bring a blanket, share a picnic, and groove under the stars — a classic summer concert date.

title: I Am King: A Tribute to Men in Music
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Father's Day-weekend tribute concert with dinner + wine + a soul/R&B show. Seated, warm, easy Sunday format with food and drinks built in.

title: Vegan Soul Sunday — Real Men Cook Vegan (Father's Day Edition)
location: New Life Presbyterian Church, 1070 Park Ave W NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Plant-based soul-food tasting event for Father's Day. Discover new vegetarian/vegan spots together while supporting Black chefs — a great foodie date with purpose.

title: Lyle Lovett — 'Songs & Stories' (WABE City Winery Series)
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: An intimate, song-and-storytelling evening with Lyle Lovett, presented with WABE. Splurge date night — quiet tables, fine wine, and legendary songwriting up close.

title: Dinner and a Date: A Night of Soul Live Music Show
description: The date-night formula built into the title: dinner + live soul music at Apache XLR. Effortless Sunday-funday send-off for the weekend.

title: This Is Atlanta — Landmark Group Exhibition
description: New 30+ artist group show celebrating Atlanta and the American South, running June 11 – July 25. Quiet, conversational, free — a perfect 'we have something to talk about on the drive home' date.

title: 'From Afrique, With Love' — Artemus Jenkins Solo Exhibition
description: Atlanta photographer Artemus Jenkins' new solo show — intimate portraiture rooted in African diasporic love. Pair with dinner in the neighborhood for a thoughtful, low-cost date.

title: Erin Gregory — 'The Butterfly Garden' Solo Exhibition
location: Anne Irwin Fine Art, Atlanta, GA
description: Vibrant floral and pollinator-themed solo show. Color-soaked and romantic — wander hand-in-hand through the gallery, then debrief over coffee.
